title: Encoder Dead-Reckoning Navigator with Kalman Heading
description: Indoor mobile robot using quadrature encoder odometry, IMU yaw fusion via 1D Kalman filter, OLED map display, and UART waypoint commands with out-of-bounds fault halt and JSON pose streaming.
objective: Navigate to UART waypoints with <5 cm error over 3 m straight runs; reduce heading drift 10× vs encoder-only; halt on OOB fault.
constraints: Arduino Mega; 600 PPR encoders on INT0-INT3; ICM-42688-P SPI CS=10; 128x64 OLED I2C; 115200 UART.
supporting_fact_titles: Wheel Odometry Dead-Reckoning and Kalman Heading Fusion
Stepper Motor Closed-Loop Control via Magnetic Encoder Feedback

element_usage_notes.ICM-42688-P High-Rate IMU via SPI: Yaw rate integrated to heading measurement for Kalman correction at 50 Hz.
element_usage_notes.Quadrature Encoder ISR Decoder: Provides left/right tick deltas for odometry pose and Kalman heading process input.
element_usage_notes.1D Kalman Heading Filter Module: Fuses encoder Δθ and IMU yaw; corrected heading used for x/y pose integration.
element_usage_notes.128x64 SSD1306 Map Renderer: Renders robot trail and position on 8x8 grid at 1 Hz.
element_usage_notes.UART Waypoint Command Parser: Sets navigation goals; streams JSON pose at 2 Hz; 'R' resets odometry.

element_interactions.description: Encoder heading delta is Kalman process input each 20 ms cycle.
IMU yaw measurement corrects encoder heading drift in Kalman update step.
Corrected pose rendered on OLED map at 1 Hz.
'R' command resets encoder accumulators and full odometry state.
